title: lupinus albicaulis var. hyacinthinus
intro: Lupinus albicaulis var. hyacinthinus, also known as the subalpine lupine, is a variant of the subalpine lupine plant species, which is a perennial flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of the western United States. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues such as diarrhea and dysentery, due to its high concentration of alkaloids and saponins. The health benefits of Lupinus albicaulis var. hyacinthinus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Traditionally, the plant's roots and flowers were used to prepare herbal remedies, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although modern uses often involve extracting its bioactive compounds for medicinal purposes.
